description: L2/microstructure strategy backlog — next candidates for the L2 backtest battery. Strategies already tested (OFI Continuation/Reversal/Microprice, CVD VWAP/Microprice/Slope/Acceleration, Sweep Continuation/Absorption/SessionHL, Absorption Reversal, CVD Absorption, Repeated Replenishment, Depth Imbalance Momentum/MeanRev, MultiTF OFI) are NOT listed here.

entries.hypothesis: When L1, L3, and L5 OFI are all aligned in the same direction, the signal is more reliable than any single level alone. Multi-level OFI confirmation reduces false signals from noise at a single price level.
entries.entry_logic: Enter long when OFI_L1, OFI_L3, OFI_L5 all in top N-th percentile simultaneously. Enter short when all in bottom N-th percentile.
entries.exit_logic: ATR-based stop (1.0x), target (rr_ratio x stop), hold_bars timeout
entries.risk_model: Same as existing L2 strategies: 1-tick slippage + $4.50 RT commission
lookahead_risks: Rolling percentile uses only past data; no future bar close
expected_failure_modes: Multi-level alignment too rare → low trade count
Correlation between levels → no added signal over single level

entries.hypothesis: When a price sweep (large directional move + high volume) is immediately followed by an OFI reversal in the opposite direction, the sweep has been absorbed and a short-term mean reversion follows.
entries.entry_logic: On bar N: detect sweep (buy_sweeps or sell_sweeps above threshold). On bar N+1 to N+3: if OFI shows opposite-direction extreme, enter fade trade.
entries.exit_logic: ATR stop, rr_ratio target, hold_bars timeout
lookahead_risks: Must not peek at bar N+1 to define bar N signal
expected_failure_modes: OFI response too slow for meaningful entry

entries.hypothesis: When price approaches a large bid or ask wall (extreme depth at a specific level) and fails to break through, a bounce occurs. Large passive orders act as price magnets and then push price back.
entries.entry_logic: Detect bars where price touches but does not exceed a large depth level. Enter fade on next bar if no breakout. Requires L10 or L5 depth columns.
entries.exit_logic: Stop beyond the wall level, target = rr_ratio × stop
lookahead_risks: Depth values must be from current bar only
expected_failure_modes: GC/SI don't have level-by-level depth in current columns — only aggregate depth imbalance. Requires mbp-10 level parse.

entries.hypothesis: When one side of the book is rapidly replenished after being consumed (absorption), the market maker defending that level signals strong directional conviction. Continue in direction of replenishment.
entries.entry_logic: Detect high absorption_score + price stable at level + spread returning to normal. Enter in direction of the absorbing side.
expected_failure_modes: RepeatedReplenishment strategy already covers similar ground — may duplicate

entries.hypothesis: When a sweep happens and the consumed side is NOT replenished (absorption_score low, book stays thin), the sweep initiates a sustained trend. Trend continuation after unreplenished sweep.
entries.entry_logic: Detect sweep (net_sweeps extreme) + absorption_score below threshold on next bar. Enter in sweep direction.
entries.exit_logic: ATR stop behind entry, trend hold_bars

entries.hypothesis: When price breaks a recent high/low but immediately shows high absorption_score (aggressive volume absorbed by passive), the breakout is false. Fade the failed breakout.
entries.entry_logic: Bar closes above N-bar high OR below N-bar low AND absorption_score in top percentile → fade (enter opposite direction)

entries.hypothesis: CVD divergence is most meaningful when it occurs at or near VWAP. At VWAP, order flow imbalance signals institutional positioning, not just random noise. Already partially tested (CVD_VWAP is a survivor), but direct divergence (price vs CVD disagreement) at VWAP not tested.
entries.entry_logic: Price at VWAP band + CVD moving opposite to recent price direction. Enter in CVD direction.
entries.notes: CVD_VWAP already tested and is a top survivor. This variant uses divergence explicitly rather than CVD percentile.

entries.hypothesis: CVD divergence at prior session high/low (key decision points) provides stronger signal than mid-range CVD divergence.
entries.entry_logic: Price at prior session high/low ± tolerance AND CVD divergence present. Enter fade or continuation depending on CVD direction.
expected_failure_modes: Prior session HL needs computation from 17:00 reset logic

entries.hypothesis: When price deviates from VWAP by more than 1 ATR and CVD diverges from the price direction, the deviation will mean-revert. Classic VWAP stretch reversal with microstructure confirmation.
entries.entry_logic: Price > VWAP + 1*ATR AND CVD trend opposing price → short. Price < VWAP - 1*ATR AND CVD trend opposite → long.

entries.hypothesis: A 30-minute directional bias filter reduces false signals from 1-minute OFI entries. Only take OFI continuation trades in the direction of the 30m trend.
entries.entry_logic: 30m bar: compute trend (e.g., price vs 10-bar MA). 1m bar: OFI in top/bottom N-th percentile AND aligned with 30m trend → enter.
entries.data_notes: Requires joining 30m bars with 1m bars. 30m bars available at 01_data/tick_bars/GC_bars_30m.parquet.
expected_failure_modes: Look-ahead: must use COMPLETED 30m bar at time of 1m signal

entries.hypothesis: NQ is a risk-on instrument that tends to lead ES at turning points. When NQ OFI signals a direction before ES, trading ES in that direction yields higher win rate.
entries.entry_logic: NQ OFI in top/bottom percentile on bar N. ES enters same direction on bar N+1.
entries.data_notes: Requires both NQ and ES bar files to be L2-enriched. Currently only GC/SI have L2 bars.
entries.blocking_reason: ES and NQ do not yet have L2 feature bars. Requires ES/NQ mbp-10 historical data.

entries.hypothesis: When both GC and SI OFI agree on direction, the signal is stronger. Gold-Silver correlation means aligned microstructure flow = institutional conviction.
entries.entry_logic: Both GC and SI OFI in same direction in top/bottom percentile simultaneously. Enter GC (or SI) trade.
entries.data_notes: Both GC and SI L2 bars exist. Needs join on timestamp.
expected_failure_modes: Correlation too high — confirmation doesn't add independence
Timing mismatch between GC and SI signals
